# Questions in Livestream 69

Live Stream #69: They Might Be Giants.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=600" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:00</a> | Did Abraham likely use images in worship, and are there ancient Near Eastern examples of worship without images? | He said Abraham probably did use images, since that was normal for the period, while truly imageless worship does appear only sparsely, with a few unusual Egyptian examples like offerings before empty shrines or thrones. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:19</a> | What is the third heaven like metaphysically, and are space and time there only conceptual? | He said heaven still seems to involve causality, sequence, and a kind of space, but not under the same framework as ordinary physical space-time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=1034" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:14</a> | Do Judas Iscariot and Cain play central roles in real modern vampire-cult mythology? | He said Judas does not, but Cain sometimes does, especially through interpretations of the mark of Cain as a vampiric sign in some modern occult or vampire-religion circles. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=1361" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:41</a> | Is Seth in Genesis supposed to be Set, the Hyksos god? | No. He said the names are not really related, Set was an older Egyptian storm god later equated with Baal, and Genesis's Seth is Abel's replacement in the good line. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=1631" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:11</a> | Is there proof in Genesis that the serpent is Satan? | Not in Genesis by itself. He said the identification comes from later biblical passages, especially Revelation 12:9, with Ezekiel 28 sometimes discussed alongside that theme. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=1881" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:21</a> | Were the Psalms always divided into five books, and why did Jesus give Mary to John rather than James? | He said the five-book Psalms division looks Masoretic, while earlier psalm collections were more fluid; he also said Jesus entrusted Mary to John because James did not yet believe and John had remained faithful at the cross. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=2279" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:59</a> | Do fringe alternative theories like Graham Hancock's help widen archaeology's scope? | No. He said such theories add confusion rather than useful scholarship, and he grouped them with other fringe or charlatan claims that ignore contrary evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=2467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:07</a> | If Sheol is not a place of punishment, why is the rich man suffering in Luke 16? | He distinguished torment from final punishment and treated the passage as describing agony in Hades rather than the last judgment itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=2760" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:00</a> | Does Proverbs 8 support the Trinity, and is Wisdom there Jesus despite being personified as feminine? | He said no. In his reading, Proverbs 8 personifies wisdom as an idealized woman rather than presenting a literal divine person of the Trinity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=3041" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:41</a> | Why are Yeshua and Satan both called the morning star? | He said the morning star is a role-symbol tied to Venus and the dawn; Satan lost that role through rebellion, while Christ fulfills it as the herald of the coming new age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=3490" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:10</a> | Did Yeshua meet Peter before the miracle of the fish in John 1:35-42? | He leaned yes from John's sequence, but said he had not studied closely enough to be fully certain how it aligns with the fishing-miracle account. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=3814" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:34</a> | Is the rapture biblical? | He said the idea is biblical in the broad sense that it comes from biblical texts, but he rejected the standard pre-tribulational Left Behind style system as resting on too many assumptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=4226" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:26</a> | Does salvation make Abraham's bosom obsolete, and can people still go there when they die? | He said Abraham's bosom no longer serves a purpose after Christ's redemptive work, so believers now go directly to heaven rather than to that intermediate compartment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=4377" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:57</a> | Why did Egypt not have good food security, and what about nobles, pharaohs, and other ancient Near Eastern cultures? | He said Egypt's food security depended heavily on Nile inundation levels driven by East African rains; even nobles could face ration limits, while Mesopotamia had more consistent rainfall and easier food planning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=4809" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:09</a> | What pattern should be noticed across the listed passages about Ham, Canaan, Abraham, Isaac, and David? | He said he could not clearly see the intended pattern in the live setting, though he recognized a weak possible link involving adultery and exposing another man's nakedness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=5138" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:38</a> | Are the twelve gems in Revelation an ancient Near Eastern motif? | He connected them chiefly to the twelve stones on the high priest's breastplate and the twelve tribes of Israel, saying the specific stones differ but the symbolic number matters. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=5345" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:05</a> | What does Proverbs 3:5-6 really mean? | He said it does not forbid understanding or reason; it warns against relying on one's own understanding as ultimate instead of trusting God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=5817" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:57</a> | Is feet washing a euphemism for sex in Genesis 19:2? | No. He said washing feet is a hospitality practice, though he separately noted that raising a garment above the ankle could carry sexual overtones in some contexts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=6051" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:51</a> | Are the signs mentioned by Josephus, Tacitus, and Cassius Dio around AD 60-70 embellishments or historical observations? | He said he had not studied those particular reports closely enough, but he tends to treat recent-event signs as possibly observational while allowing that some could be literary embellishment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=6312" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:12</a> | Could the Cave of the Patriarchs or the traditional tomb of Aaron be evidence for Abraham or Aaron? | He dismissed Aaron's tomb tradition as highly doubtful and treated Abraham's cave only as weak circumstantial evidence supported by tradition and broadly fitting archaeology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=6528" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:48</a> | If kosher laws no longer bind Christians, how should Isaiah 66:15-17 be understood when it condemns eating swine and mice? | He read the passage as judgment on idolatrous rebellion rather than as proof that new-covenant believers remain under kosher law. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=7149" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:09</a> | Which other scholars date the Pentateuch early and in Egypt because of cultural similarities, and why do mainstream scholars date it late? | He named Benjamin Noonan, Joshua Berman, Alan Millard, James Hoffmeier, and Chris McKinny, then said many mainstream scholars remain shaped by the documentary hypothesis and often ignore or explain away Egyptian details. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=7389" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:09</a> | What are his thoughts on Deuteronomy 23:1-3 as ritual cleanliness rather than moral standards? | He agreed that the passage concerns ritual purity and access to the assembly or sacred space, not moral guilt for bodily condition, birth status, or ethnicity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=7769" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:29</a> | What does he think of identifying Babel with Eridu in Iraq? | He was skeptical and said the Eridu proposal depends on exceptionally thin and fragmentary evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=8020" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:40</a> | Can the JEP or documentary hypothesis be applied to the Septuagint or other ancient Near Eastern literature? | He said scholars have tried applying it to the Septuagint with mixed results, and the outcome depends heavily on which criteria, such as doublets or changes in person, are used. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=8306" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:26</a> | Why do some scholars reject the idea that the Hyksos were ancestors of the early Israelites? | He said Hyksos rulers and the later Israelites should not simply be equated: both relate to Semitic populations at Avaris, but they were not the same group and `Israelites` is itself anachronistic before the Exodus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=8640" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:00</a> | What does he think of Angela Mazzifra's article on a sixteenth-century BC destruction layer at Hazor? | He said he would need to read the article and site report before judging it; as described, the layer would be Middle Bronze and too early for most Exodus chronologies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=8810" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:50</a> | Which Egyptian god is best, and why is it Thoth? | He agreed with Thoth, citing roles tied to scribes, writing, magic, the moon, medicine, a broad cult, and the unusual involvement of commoners in Thoth's service. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=9213" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:33</a> | What is the estimated timeline for his book about the ten plagues? | He said it is still a long way off because writing it would require substantial uninterrupted time or sabbatical support, though he still wants to produce it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=9542" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:02</a> | Was Sheshonq a political opportunist exploiting Israel's division by attacking Jeroboam's lands? | Yes. He said Sheshonq was opportunistic, used division and intrigue, and attacked both kingdoms when they were divided in order to weaken and pillage the Levant. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=9693" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:33</a> | From which Mesopotamian population was Abram descended? | He said Abram was from the Amorites of northern Mesopotamia around Urkesh, likely associated with the king of Mari rather than with southern Ur. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=9848" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:08</a> | Could the Merneptah Stele's reference to Israel's seed be a pun on Jezreel, meaning God will sow? | He said it is possible and not beyond Egyptian poetic practice, though he would need to look into the meaning and proposal further before judging it firmly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=10144" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:04</a> | How could Jesus's physical body enter heaven if heaven is a spiritual realm? | He said Scripture already has bodily ascension precedents such as Enoch and Elijah, and heaven seems to have causality, space, time, and rules that make bodily entrance not obviously incompatible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=10558" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:58</a> | Did Egyptians really use cheetahs as hunting animals? | He said they clearly kept exotic animals and used trained dogs and cats for hunting, but he was unsure whether cheetahs were actually trained for hunting and would need to check the evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=10961" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:41</a> | Why are there no hieratic or demotic Unicode fonts? | He said demotic relies on whole-word ligatures rather than simple letters, while hieratic varies by period and sign form, making both font design and character encoding difficult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/gRDrvTEmitw?t=11547" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:27</a> | Is it worth studying Paleo-Hebrew or early alphabetic inscriptions? | Yes. He strongly encouraged it, arguing that Northwest Semitic epigraphy is valuable background for biblical study and badly needs more qualified specialists. |
